steps.notes: 8/8 tests pass. 3 existing org-membership + invalid-signature tests unregressed; 5 new: user.created upsert (asserts NO Stripe call), user.updated upsert (updated_at Date), user.deleted deleteMany, malformed-payload 400 guard (boundary case), Prisma-failure 500 with no DB-detail leak (boundary case).

steps.command: corepack pnpm install && corepack pnpm turbo run build --dry
steps.notes: pnpm install regenerated lockfile, added prisma+@prisma/client, recognized `backend` (21st-dev/apps/backend) as workspace member with no errors. turbo dry-run lists backend in Packages in Scope; backend#build is a no-op (no build script — B2 documented, expected).

steps.step: C1-C3/C5 generated client excluded from git
steps.notes: Only packages/db/prisma/schema.prisma listed as untracked. The generated prisma/client/ (3.1MB) is fully excluded (git check-ignore packages/db/prisma/client confirms).
node --test 1/1 pass. Asserts PrismaClient instantiates without throwing, `local_users` delegate present, `users` delegate ABSENT (rename verified at runtime). No DB connection opened.

steps.step: apps/web full production build (root app unaffected)
steps.notes: Next.js production build succeeds. /api/webhooks/clerk route compiles with the new @repo/db/prisma import.

KNOWN GAP. An empty gitignored packages/db/.env was created, but its placeholder CONTENT could not be written: the repo privacy-block hook requires interactive user approval for any .env write that this autonomous agent cannot self-grant. The required placeholder content is documented in root .env.example (DATABASE_URL / DIRECT_DATABASE_URL, committed) — the user must populate packages/db/.env and apps/web/.env.local with real values.
